event_description: A large double-ended ferry moves through open water near island or shoreline background. EO includes strong sun glint and scale changes; IR supports the same hull and wake through the degraded EO intervals.

qa.question: In the early EO segment, which cue pairing is visible for the object?

options.A: the object with hull continuity through glint and scale change; island or shoreline background nearby
options.B: the object with island or shoreline background; hull continuity through glint and scale change nearby
options.C: the object with shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; bright water or fixed shoreline texture nearby
options.D: the object with bright water or fixed shoreline texture; wake shape next to the hull nearby

qa.evidence_note: the hull and wake survive large scale changes and glint intervals

qa.question: Across the EO scale or angle change, which motion cue stays paired with the object?

options.A: island or shoreline background; view scale or angle changes
options.B: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; view scale or angle changes
options.C: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; view scale or angle changes
options.D: hull continuity through glint and scale change; view scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: the hull and wake survive large scale changes and glint intervals

qa.question: In IR, which contrast pairing is visible near the object?

options.A: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; shoreline, glare, or open-water texture
options.B: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; island or shoreline background
options.C: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; hull continuity through glint and scale change
options.D: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; bright water or fixed shoreline texture

qa.evidence_note: IR preserves the same hull and wake when EO water contrast is degraded

qa.question: Which nearby cue creates a same-scene confusion?

options.A: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; near the object
options.B: hull continuity through glint and scale change; near the object
options.C: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; near the object
options.D: island or shoreline background; near the object

qa.evidence_note: treating glare intervals as a target disappearance or replacement

qa.question: Which EO/IR cue pair is visible?

options.A: EO: hull continuity through glint and scale change; IR: bright water or fixed shoreline texture
options.B: EO: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.C: EO: island or shoreline background; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.D: EO: hull continuity through glint and scale change; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast

qa.evidence_note: EO: the hull and wake survive large scale changes and glint intervals; IR: IR preserves the same hull and wake when EO water contrast is degraded

qa.question: Which cue pairing continues through camera/view change?

options.A: the object: island or shoreline background; screen scale or angle changes
options.B: the object: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; screen scale or angle changes
options.C: the object: hull continuity through glint and scale change; screen scale or angle changes
options.D: the object: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; screen scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: the hull and wake survive large scale changes and glint intervals

qa.question: Which EO cue is aligned with the IR contrast cue?

options.A: EO cue: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; IR cue: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.B: EO cue: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; IR cue: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.C: EO cue: hull continuity through glint and scale change; IR cue: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.D: EO cue: island or shoreline background; IR cue: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast

qa.evidence_note: EO: the hull and wake survive large scale changes and glint intervals; IR: IR preserves the same hull and wake when EO water contrast is degraded

qa.question: Which competing cue appears in the cross-modal chain?

options.A: EO/visible cue: island or shoreline background; IR/contrast context: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.B: EO/visible cue: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; IR/contrast context: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.C: EO/visible cue: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; IR/contrast context: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.D: EO/visible cue: hull continuity through glint and scale change; IR/contrast context: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast

qa.evidence_note: treating glare intervals as a target disappearance or replacement

qa.question: When the view changes, which cue is checked against the nearby alternative?

options.A: island or shoreline background; hull continuity through glint and scale change
options.B: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; hull continuity through glint and scale change
options.C: hull continuity through glint and scale change; island or shoreline background
options.D: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; island or shoreline background

qa.evidence_note: the hull and wake survive large scale changes and glint intervals

qa.question: Which IR cue pairing appears in this interval?

options.A: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; island or shoreline background
options.B: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; shoreline, glare, or open-water texture
options.C: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; hull continuity through glint and scale change
options.D: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; bright water or fixed shoreline texture

qa.evidence_note: IR preserves the same hull and wake when EO water contrast is degraded

qa.question: Which combined EO/IR sequence account is visible?

options.A: EO: hull continuity through glint and scale change; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.B: EO: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.C: EO: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.D: EO: island or shoreline background; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast

qa.evidence_note: EO: the hull and wake survive large scale changes and glint intervals; IR: IR preserves the same hull and wake when EO water contrast is degraded

qa.question: For the full clip, which cue chain is visible?

options.A: the object: island or shoreline background; nearby cue: hull continuity through glint and scale change
options.B: the object: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; nearby cue: hull continuity through glint and scale change
options.C: the object: hull continuity through glint and scale change; nearby cue: island or shoreline background
options.D: the object: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; nearby cue: island or shoreline background

qa.evidence_note: treating glare intervals as a target disappearance or replacement

group_scoring_recommendation.C1: Use squared-ratio consistency scoring for the eight-step parallel cue chain.
group_scoring_recommendation.H1: Prefer prefix scoring because the four questions form a cue-chain verdict.

notes.annotation_visibility_warning: Do not export main_event, event_description, answer, option_roles, evidence_note, rationales, or time_reference metadata to model prompts.
notes.design_revision_note: Video-reasoning v15 text-gate repair r2: options are neutral cue-pair choices with same-scene distractors, reducing answerability from wording alone.
acceptance_gates: Run request key-path leakage audit after export.
Run Qwen text-only/no-video baseline before video inference.
Reject samples or templates whose text-only accuracy suggests wording shortcuts.
Keep evidence_note fields internal; they are for annotation audit only.
